scenario: GitOps render-vs-live drift — `helm template` expected state (replicas=3, memory=512Mi) vs observed cluster (replicas=1, memory=128Mi, OOMKilled)
notes: Unlike h001-h011 (values.yaml vs stored helm release values), this case proves the anchor-by-render path: the EXPECTED state is reconstructed by rendering the chart with `helm template` (committed at rendered/expected.yaml), then diffed against the OBSERVED live cluster. The deterministic test diffs the committed rendered manifest (no helm binary required); the helm-guarded test re-renders the chart and asserts it still matches the committed golden.
